text:
Once upon a time, in a forest shrouded in mist where the sunlight could barely penetrate, there lived a deer with no knees. Every day, she’d wander slowly down a moss-strewn path that led her to the forest’s edge. She enjoyed the soft kiss of morning dew on her antlers. Today, after a long morning of grazing on wild berries, she thought she’d stop at a little pond she’d gotten acquainted with.

The pond was a mirror image of the sky, dotted with soft pink wildflowers. She’d listened to the soft sounds of the forest, observed owl owlets growing feathers, and sometimes, just sat there feeling utterly content.

Then, suddenly, she heard it—the cheerful chirp of a robin. She’d never noticed one before. The robin landed on the water’s edge and perched above her antlers. It hummed a familiar song. Surely it wouldn’t hurt to talk?

She flicked her long, curious tail leaf by leaf
